title: linaria alpina subsp. faucicola
intro: Linaria alpina subsp. faucicola, also known as the Faucolic Toadflax, is a rare and endemic plant species found in the mountainous regions of the Iberian Peninsula. This perennial plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, with its aerial parts possessing medicinal properties such as antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral activities. The health benefits of Linaria alpina subsp. faucicola include its potential to alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections, such as coughs and colds, as well as its ability to reduce inflammation and improve wound healing. In herbal preparations, the plant can be used to make teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be consumed orally to harness its medicinal properties.
